explanations.1: This block sets up a virtual host that listens on port 80 (HTTP) for all network interfaces; the asterisk means 'accept connections on any IP address'.
explanations.6: AllowOverride All lets Apache read .htaccess files in this directory, which are little config files that can override the main Apache settings.
explanations.8: This checks if the requested file doesn't actually exist on the server (the !-f means 'not a file'); it's used to detect requests for routes that don't correspond to real files.
explanations.9: If a request is for something that isn't a real file, this rule rewrites it to send index.html instead while preserving the original query string parameters (QSA means 'query string append').
